title: Citrix NetScaler ADC & Gateway Critical Memory Overread (CVE-2026-3055) Under Active Exploitation — SAML IDP Configurations Leaking Sensitive Data

sources.name: Rapid7 - CVE-2026-3055: Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway Out-of-Bounds Read
Help Net Security - Critical NetScaler ADC, Gateway flaw may soon be exploited (CVE-2026-3055)
Qualys ThreatPROTECT - Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway Multiple Vulnerabilities
Security Affairs - Urgent Alert: NetScaler bug CVE-2026-3055 probed by attackers
Infosecurity Magazine - Critical Citrix NetScaler Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
Cybersecurity News - Hackers Probe Citrix NetScaler Instances Ahead of Likely Exploitation
The Cyber Express - CVE-2026-3055 Threatens Citrix NetScaler SAML IDP
Born's Tech - Vulnerability CVE-2026-3055 in Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway is Being Exploited
Vulert - Citrix NetScaler Under Active Recon for CVE-2026-3055

timeline.event: Citrix publishes security advisory for CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368 affecting NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway products
Rapid7 and Qualys publish detailed analysis; security community begins assessing exposure across internet-facing NetScaler appliances
watchTowr confirms active reconnaissance against NetScaler instances via Attacker Eye honeypot network; known threat actor source IPs observed probing for CVE-2026-3055
Multiple reports confirm in-the-wild exploitation has begun; organizations with SAML IDP configurations are primary targets for sensitive data exfiltration

simple_summary.what_happened: Citrix disclosed a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-3055) in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to read sensitive data directly from the appliance's memory. The flaw is caused by insufficient input validation leading to a memory overread condition. Systems configured as SAML Identity Providers are vulnerable. Within days of disclosure, security researchers confirmed active exploitation attempts from known threat actor IP addresses, with attackers scanning the internet for vulnerable NetScaler instances to steal authentication tokens, session data, and other secrets from device memory.
simple_summary.who_is_affected: Organizations running on-premise NetScaler ADC or NetScaler Gateway appliances configured as SAML Identity Providers are vulnerable. Affected versions include NetScaler ADC and Gateway 14.1 before 14.1-66.59 and 13.1 before 13.1-62.23. Default configurations that do not use SAML IDP are not affected. Given NetScaler's widespread deployment in enterprise environments for load balancing, VPN, and authentication, thousands of internet-facing appliances across government, financial services, healthcare, and large enterprises are potentially exposed.
simple_summary.what_attackers_do: Step 1: Attackers scan the internet for exposed NetScaler ADC and Gateway appliances using specialized tools and Shodan/Censys queries to identify systems with SAML IDP configurations enabled. Step 2: The attacker sends a specially crafted unauthenticated request to the SAML IDP endpoint that triggers an out-of-bounds memory read due to insufficient input validation. Step 3: The appliance returns more data than intended from its memory, potentially including SAML authentication tokens, session cookies, private keys, configuration secrets, and other sensitive data stored in adjacent memory regions. Step 4: Attackers use the leaked authentication material to gain unauthorized access to backend applications, impersonate legitimate users, or pivot further into the network infrastructure.
what_you_should_do: Immediately upgrade NetScaler ADC and Gateway to fixed versions: 14.1-66.59 or later for the 14.1 branch, and 13.1-62.23 or later for the 13.1 branch

Check if your appliance is configured as a SAML IDP by searching your configuration for 'add authentication samlIdPProfile' — if not present, your default configuration is not vulnerable to CVE-2026-3055
Review NetScaler access logs for anomalous SAML endpoint requests, especially from unexpected source IPs or with malformed parameters, since March 23, 2026
If exploitation is suspected, rotate all SAML signing certificates, invalidate active sessions, and rotate any secrets or tokens that may have been stored in appliance memory
Restrict management and SAML endpoint access to trusted IP ranges using network ACLs or firewall rules as an interim mitigation if immediate patching is not possible
simple_summary.why_this_is_serious: This vulnerability is critical because NetScaler appliances sit at the perimeter of enterprise networks and handle sensitive authentication traffic. A memory overread vulnerability in the SAML IDP component can expose authentication tokens, session secrets, and private keys — giving attackers the ability to impersonate legitimate users and bypass authentication entirely. The fact that exploitation requires no authentication and is confirmed active in the wild makes this an emergency-level patch priority. Citrix NetScaler has been a high-value target for threat actors historically, and delayed patching of previous NetScaler vulnerabilities has led to significant breaches across critical infrastructure.

tactics.description: Attackers exploit the CVE-2026-3055 vulnerability in internet-facing NetScaler ADC/Gateway SAML IDP endpoints to read sensitive data from appliance memory without authentication
Memory overread condition exposes SAML tokens, session cookies, and potentially private keys from NetScaler appliance memory, enabling credential theft
Threat actors actively scanning internet-facing NetScaler instances to identify appliances with SAML IDP configurations vulnerable to CVE-2026-3055

tactics.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
tactics.description: Stolen SAML tokens and session cookies from memory overread can be used to access backend applications and services authenticated through the compromised NetScaler

detection_guidance.summary: Detection should focus on identifying anomalous requests to SAML IDP endpoints on NetScaler appliances, monitoring for reconnaissance scanning activity, and auditing authentication logs for signs of token reuse or session hijacking. Organizations should also verify their NetScaler firmware versions against the advisory.

log_sources.description: Monitor ns.log and nsvpn.log for unusual SAML endpoint requests, especially malformed or oversized requests to authentication endpoints from untrusted IP addresses

Monitor inbound connections to NetScaler SAML IDP endpoints for scanning patterns: rapid sequential requests from single IPs, requests from known threat actor infrastructure, or connections from Tor exit nodes
Audit SAML assertion logs for token reuse, impossible travel scenarios, or authentication from unexpected source IPs that could indicate stolen token usage
Cross-reference source IPs of NetScaler management and SAML endpoint connections against known threat actor infrastructure and watchTowr honeypot intelligence

what_to_look_for.indicator: Malformed or oversized HTTP requests to SAML IDP endpoints
what_to_look_for.description: Requests to SAML authentication endpoints with unusual parameters or oversized payloads may indicate exploitation attempts targeting the memory overread

what_to_look_for.indicator: Rapid sequential requests from single IPs to SAML endpoints
what_to_look_for.description: Automated scanning tools sending multiple requests in rapid succession to identify vulnerable NetScaler instances with SAML IDP enabled

what_to_look_for.indicator: SAML token reuse from unexpected IP addresses
what_to_look_for.description: Authentication tokens obtained from memory leak being replayed from different IP addresses than the original session
Any NetScaler ADC or Gateway running versions prior to 14.1-66.59 or 13.1-62.23 with SAML IDP configuration is vulnerable

immediate_actions.description: Upgrade NetScaler ADC and Gateway to 14.1-66.59+ or 13.1-62.23+ immediately to remediate CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368
Determine if your appliance is configured as a SAML IDP — if not, the default configuration is not vulnerable to CVE-2026-3055
As an interim mitigation, restrict access to SAML IDP endpoints to trusted IP ranges using ACLs or firewall rules

immediate_actions.action: Audit authentication logs for compromise indicators
immediate_actions.description: Review NetScaler access logs and downstream SAML authentication logs for anomalous activity since March 23, 2026

immediate_actions.action: Rotate SAML certificates and secrets if exploitation suspected
immediate_actions.description: If evidence of exploitation is found, rotate all SAML signing certificates, invalidate active sessions, and regenerate any secrets stored on the appliance
